data.title: Bel Fuse (BELF.A) Quarterly Loss Tests Bullish Earnings Growth Narrative
data.description:
Bel Fuse (BELF.A) has wrapped up FY 2025 with Q4 revenue of US$175.9 million and a basic EPS loss of US$0.43, while trailing twelve month figures show revenue of US$675.5 million and EPS of US$4.87, underpinned by trailing earnings growth of 50.2% year over year. The company has seen revenue step up from US$534.8 million on a trailing basis in Q4 2024 to US$675.5 million in Q4 2025, with net income rising from US$41.0 million to US$61.5 million over the same period, setting the stage for investors to weigh improved margins and earnings trends as the key takeaway from this latest update.

See our full analysis for Bel Fuse.

With the numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this performance lines up against the main stories circulating around the stock, and where the data either backs or challenges those narratives.

See what the community is saying about Bel Fuse

Margins Hold Up At 9.1%

  • Bel Fuse is earning US$61.5 million of trailing net income on US$675.5 million of revenue, which lines up with the 9.1% net margin cited in the analysis and compares to 7.7% a year earlier.
  • Consensus narrative points to cost programs and mix helping profitability, and the numbers partly back that up:
    • Trailing earnings grew 50.2% year over year and five year annual earnings growth is 19.4%, which is consistent with a business that has been able to support higher margins over time.
    • At the same time, the consensus view highlights margin volatility in the Power segment and tariff related pressures, and the Q4 FY 2025 loss of US$5.4 million shows margins are not yet smooth across every period.

Earnings Growth Outruns Revenue

  • Trailing revenue sits at US$675.5 million while trailing net income is US$61.5 million, and over the last year earnings grew 50.2% compared with forecast revenue growth of 6.6% per year highlighted in the analysis.
  • Bulls focus on earnings power more than top line growth, and the data gives them some backing and some pushback:
    • The 19.4% annual earnings growth over five years and the 18.4% forecast earnings growth rate both indicate that profit has been growing faster than the revenue line cited at 6.6% growth.
    • However, Q4 FY 2025 shows a loss of US$5.4 million compared with profits in the prior three quarters, which suggests earnings can still swing even as the longer term averages look strong.

    • The stock sits about 7% below the cited DCF fair value of US$222.33, yet still carries a higher P/E than peers and the industry, which is unusual for a company priced at a discount to a DCF model.
    • With revenue projected to grow at 6.6% a year versus the 10.3% US market benchmark, skeptics can point to slower revenue growth alongside the premium P/E as a reason to be careful about how much optimism is already in the price.

data.title: Tariff Cuts And US Investments Reframe TSMC Growth And Valuation Story
data.description:
• The US and Taiwan have agreed to lower tariffs on Taiwanese imports, directly affecting Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing’s export terms into the US market.
  • The Taiwanese government has pledged new investments in US semiconductor, energy, and AI sectors, with major commitments from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing.
  • These moves mark a shift in cross border cooperation that could influence how semiconductor supply chains and technologies are structured globally.

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing, NYSE:TSM, comes into this trade and investment reset with a recent share price of $362.26 and a 1 year return of 82.2%. Over 3 years, its return is about 4.3x, and over 5 years it stands at 192.4%. That backdrop helps explain why changes to tariff treatment and overseas commitments attract close attention from investors.

The lower US tariffs and fresh US focused investment plans could influence how Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing positions its production, partnerships, and capital spending over time. For you as an investor, the key questions now center on how these policy shifts might affect the company’s access to US demand, its cost structure, and its role in future semiconductor and AI build outs.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing.

We've flagged 1 risk for Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing. See which could impact your investment.

The tariff cut from 20% to 15% directly reduces the cost of TSMC’s chips entering the US, which could improve its price competitiveness versus foundry peers such as Samsung Electronics and Intel. Combined with Taiwan’s pledge to invest more than US$250b in US semiconductor, energy, and AI production, including TSMC’s US$100b commitment, this agreement ties the company more closely to US AI and high performance computing build outs. For a contract foundry that relies on large, long term customer relationships, closer alignment with US policy and capital spending could support order visibility, especially as AI compute demand remains a central theme.

For you, the trade off to think about is that bigger US exposure may also come with higher fixed costs, execution complexity across multiple geographies, and potential political expectations around future capacity allocation. The agreement could influence where TSMC places its most advanced nodes versus mature-node production, how it prices wafers for different customers, and how its multi year capex plans balance between Taiwan and overseas fabs. Execution on the large US projects, supply chain reliability, and any shifts in customer mix across AI and non AI demand are likely to be key focus points after this announcement.

Advertisement

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Large US-focused capex programs can strain execution and may pressure returns if construction or ramp timelines slip.
  • ⚠️ Analysts have flagged that TSMC has a high level of non cash earnings, which can make reported profit quality harder to interpret.
  • 🎁 Earnings are forecast to grow 17.47% per year, suggesting analysts see room for further profit expansion alongside these trade and investment moves.
  • 🎁 TSMC’s P/E of 28.9x is below the 42.3x semiconductor industry average and the company is described as trading at good value compared to peers and industry, which some investors may view as supportive if the new agreement helps sustain demand.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, you may want to watch how TSMC phases its US$100b US investment, including the timing of fab build outs, advanced-node roadmaps, and any updates on cost-sharing or incentives from US authorities. Keep an eye on US versus non US revenue mix, margins from overseas fabs compared with Taiwan, and whether AI-related partnerships translate into long term, contract-backed capacity commitments. Any further policy shifts between the US, Taiwan, and other chipmaking regions, as well as capex plans from large customers such as US hyperscalers, will also matter for how this agreement ultimately feeds into TSMC’s earnings profile and capital intensity.

data.title: Hackett Group (HCKT) Margin Compression Challenges Bullish Gen AI Earnings Narrative
data.description:
Advertisement

Hackett Group FY 2025 earnings snapshot

Hackett Group (HCKT) has wrapped up FY 2025 with fourth quarter revenue of about US$75.8 million and basic EPS of US$0.21, alongside trailing twelve month revenue of US$305.6 million and EPS of US$0.47 that sit against a net profit margin of 4.2% for the period. Over recent quarters, the company has seen revenue move between US$72.2 million and US$77.6 million, while quarterly EPS ranged from US$0.06 to US$0.31 and trailing EPS shifted from US$1.23 to US$0.47, creating a picture where current profitability metrics are compared with forecasts for rapid earnings growth and softer margins.

See our full analysis for Hackett Group.

With the headline results on the table, the next step is to see how these numbers line up against the prevailing market narratives around Hackett Group, including views on its growth potential, margin profile, and overall risk reward balance.

See what the community is saying about Hackett Group

Margins Compress as Net Profit Halves Year on Year

  • Trailing 12 month net income dropped from US$29.6 million a year ago to US$12.9 million, with net margin moving from 9.7% to 4.2%, even though revenue stayed close to US$305 million over both periods.
  • Critics highlight that weaker profitability complicates the bullish view that Gen AI investments will lift margins, because:
    • Analysts currently expect profit margins to move from 5.5% to 16.4% over three years, yet the latest trailing data points the other way, at 4.2% versus 9.7% last year.
    • Management is still dealing with higher SG&A and restructuring costs, while legacy areas like Oracle Solutions are described as facing a 20%+ year on year revenue hit. This aligns more with the cautious narrative than with a clean margin expansion story.

Premium 29.5x P/E With Debt And Weak Dividend Cover

  • The shares trade on a 29.5x P/E versus 22.5x for the US IT industry and 25.4x for peers. The dividend yield is 3.15% but described as not well covered by earnings, and the company is flagged as carrying a high level of debt.
  • Bears argue this richer multiple is hard to justify given the balance sheet and payout profile, because:
    • Leverage is called out as elevated at the same time as net margin sits at 4.2%, so the business is running with thinner profit cushions while also supporting interest and principal obligations.
    • The dividend, at 3.15%, is specifically tagged as weakly covered by earnings, which aligns with the cautious view that current profitability leaves less room for error if growth or Gen AI monetization does not arrive as expected.
Bears watching this valuation and payout mix are asking whether income and margin headroom are enough for a 29.5x P/E story. 🐻 Hackett Group Bear Case

DCF Fair Value Signals Upside Versus Current Price

  • While the current share price is US$15.22, the stock is assessed as trading about 51% below a DCF fair value of US$31.06, and analysts are also quoted as expecting earnings to grow about 45.8% per year over the next three years on revenue growth of 1.4% per year.
  • Supporters of the bullish view lean on this valuation gap and earnings ramp, yet the numbers invite a closer look, because:
    • The same data set that points to US$31.06 DCF fair value also shows trailing EPS sliding from US$1.23 to US$0.47 over the last year, which is very different from the forecast path to US$1.87 EPS by around 2028.
    • Consensus narrative credits AI XPLR, ZBrain and related Gen AI offerings with reshaping the revenue mix toward higher margin, recurring streams, but the latest 4.2% net margin and modest revenue trend of about US$300 million suggest that shift has not yet flowed through to reported profitability.
If you are weighing that US$31.06 DCF fair value against the US$15.22 share price, it is worth seeing how the bullish and cautious cases stack up side by side. 🐂 Hackett Group Bull Case
